symptoms.symptom_name: Syncope triggered by acute intense emotion with rapid full recovery

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Adrenergically-triggered syncope is a hallmark presentation of CPVT.
A palpitation prodrome and exertional pattern are atypical for benign vasovagal syncope.
A tachyarrhythmic prodrome indicates the syncope is arrhythmic rather than reflex-mediated.

symptoms.symptom_name: Family history of juvenile sudden cardiac death (cousin died at 16 playing sport)

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Juvenile sudden cardiac death in a relative markedly increases the pre-test probability of an inherited channelopathy.
The same family history supports an alternative inherited channelopathy requiring exclusion.

symptoms.symptom_name: Recurrent exertional presyncope during competitive sport over the past year

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exertional presyncope reflects prior sub-threshold catecholaminergic arrhythmic episodes.

symptoms.symptom_name: No post-ictal confusion, tongue-biting, incontinence, chest pain or breathlessness

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Rapid recovery without post-ictal features argues against a primary seizure.

symptoms.symptom_name: Bidirectional/polymorphic VT on exercise stress test with structurally normal heart and normal resting ECG

investigation_details.investigation_name: Graded exercise stress test (with resting ECG and ambulatory monitoring)
investigation_details.expected_result: Provoked bidirectional or polymorphic ventricular tachycardia at higher heart rates, with an otherwise structurally normal heart and normal resting ECG
investigation_details.if_not_ordered_consequence: CPVT remains undiagnosed and the patient is exposed to recurrent potentially fatal arrhythmic triggers.

diagnostic_weight_edges.evidence_basis: Exercise-provoked bidirectional VT with a structurally normal heart is the diagnostic signature of CPVT.

symptom_clusters.clinical_significance: Emotion/exertion-triggered syncope with a palpitation prodrome, recurrent exertional presyncope, and a family history of juvenile sudden cardiac death together form a high-probability constellation for an inherited catecholaminergic arrhythmia demanding urgent escalation and activity restriction.

investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: First-line screen (QT interval, baseline abnormalities); expected normal in CPVT but essential to exclude long QT and other causes.
Provocative test to reveal bidirectional/polymorphic VT - the diagnostic hallmark; performed under specialist supervision.
Captures exertional/emotional arrhythmic events during daily activity.
Confirms a structurally normal heart and excludes structural mimics.

investigation_recommendations_expected.investigation_name: Reassurance and discharge without cardiac investigation
investigation_recommendations_expected.rationale: Clearing this presentation without ECG, provocative testing and specialist review risks a fatal missed inherited arrhythmia.
